summary: Admin API comprehensive testing completed. All 34 tests pass. Fixed 3 bugs in admin-api.js related to incorrect Prisma field names (timestamp→createdAt, organizationId→orgId, escrowFunding→fundingEvent).

bugs_fixed.issue: GET /api/admin/metrics/activity - Used 'timestamp' instead of 'createdAt' for AuditEvent model
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed timestamp to createdAt, organizationId to orgId, metadata to details
Changed to prisma.fundingEvent with correct status 'succeeded' instead of 'completed'

bugs_fixed.issue: GET /api/admin/audit - Used incorrect field names (timestamp, organizationId, resourceType, resourceId, metadata)
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed to correct Prisma schema fields (createdAt, orgId, escrowId, details, ipAddress)

health_scope: GET /api/admin/health - Public health check (no auth) - PASS
GET /api/admin/status - Detailed system status (requires health scope) - PASS
GET /api/admin/errors - Recent error logs (requires health scope) - PASS
audit_scope: GET /api/admin/audit - Cross-org audit query - PASS (after fix)
keys_scope: POST /api/admin/keys - Create admin key with scopes - PASS
setup: POST /api/admin/setup - Fails when keys exist - PASS
scope_enforcement: Scoped key can access allowed endpoints (health, metrics) - PASS
Scoped key denied for audit scope - PASS (403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE)
Scoped key denied for blog scope - PASS (403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE)
Scoped key denied for keys scope - PASS (403 INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE)
Revoked key cannot access any endpoint - PASS (401)

seed_data_creation: Created test admin keys and blog posts during testing (cleaned up via revoke/delete)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin API fully tested with scope-based access control. All endpoints working correctly after bug fixes. Superadmin key has ['*'] scope for all access. Scoped keys correctly enforce INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE (403) for unauthorized endpoints.
